flashcards.front: What is a friend function in C++?
flashcards.back: A non-member function that has access to the private and protected members of a class.

flashcards.front: How is a friend function declared?
flashcards.back: Using the 'friend' keyword inside the class declaration: friend ReturnType functionName(Parameters);
A class whose all member functions have access to the private and protected members of another class.
No, friendship is not inherited. Derived classes do not automatically become friends of base class friends.

flashcards.front: What is a common use case for friend functions?
flashcards.back: Operator overloading, especially when the left operand is not an object of the class.
